# Durham Timetable to ICS Converter

## Migration and Implementation Plan

### Purpose

This document describes how to migrate the existing timetable-to-calendar prototype to the revised Durham-specific timetable converter specification.

The aim is to preserve useful existing functionality while replacing architectural components that no longer match the project requirements.

---

# Current State Assessment

The existing prototype already provides:

* Static browser-based architecture.
* HTML file upload and drag-and-drop support.
* Durham timetable table parsing.
* Event preview interface.
* ICS file generation.
* Room extraction.
* Durham week pattern expansion.

These components should be retained where possible.

The prototype currently differs from the revised specification in several important areas:

* Generic timetable extraction remains the primary design.
* Event titles are unsuitable.
* Academic-year definitions are not used.
* Event UIDs are random.
* ICS output uses UTC timestamps.
* Recurrence optimisation is absent.
* Teaching-week calendars are absent.

The project should therefore be treated as a substantial refactor rather than a complete rewrite.

---

# Phase 1: Refactor Internal Data Model

## Objective

Separate timetable parsing from calendar generation.

The current implementation expands directly into calendar events.

Instead introduce three logical layers:

```text
Timetable Activity
    ↓
Occurrences
    ↓
ICS Components
```

## Activity Structure

Each parsed activity should contain:

```text
moduleCode
moduleName
shortModuleName

activityCode
activityType

timetableCode

staff
location

day
startTime
duration

weekPattern
expandedWeeks
```

Example:

```text
moduleCode: MATH2761
moduleName: Statistical Inference II
shortModuleName: SI2

activityCode: LECT
activityType: Lecture

timetableCode: MATH2761/LECT/001

location: CG93

day: Tuesday
startTime: 14:00
duration: 60

weekPattern: 12-16
expandedWeeks: [12,13,14,15,16]
```

No ICS generation should occur during parsing.

---

# Phase 2: Durham Parser Consolidation

## Objective

Convert the parser into a Durham-specific parser.

The current Durham parsing logic already extracts:

* day
* start time
* duration
* timetable code
* module name
* week pattern
* room
* staff

This logic should be retained.

## Remove Generic Extraction

The generic timetable parser should be removed from the primary workflow.

The application should assume Durham timetable HTML exports.

Future support for other timetable formats can be added later as a separate parser module.

---

# Phase 3: Academic-Year Configuration System

## Objective

Move all week/date mapping into configuration.

Introduce:

```text
academicYears/
    2026-27.json
    2027-28.json
    ...
```

Each definition contains:

```text
yearLabel

week0Start

teachingWeeks

termBoundaries

moduleShortNames
```

Example:

```text
SI2 -> Statistical Inference II
```

## Academic Year Selection

The application should:

1. Attempt automatic detection.
2. Allow manual override.
3. Always use the selected academic-year definition for date generation.

No date calculations should depend on values extracted from timetable headers.

---

# Phase 4: Activity Type Mapping

## Objective

Convert timetable abbreviations into human-readable names.

Initial mappings:

| Raw Code | Display        |
| -------- | -------------- |
| LECT     | Lecture        |
| TUT      | Tutorial       |
| PRAC     | Practical      |
| PROB     | Problems Class |
| PROBA    | Problems Class |

Unknown codes should fall back to the raw value.

---

# Phase 5: Event Naming System

## Objective

Support user-selectable title formats.

### Full Name Mode

```text
Statistical Inference II: Lecture
```

### Short Name Mode

```text
SI2: Lecture
```

Short name mode should be the default.

### Optional Future Mode

```text
Lecture
```

This is not required for the initial release.

Module codes must not appear in event titles.

---

# Phase 6: Occurrence Generation

## Objective

Convert teaching weeks into dated occurrences.

The academic-year definition becomes the single source of truth.

Example:

```text
Week 12
Tuesday
14:00
```

becomes:

```text
Tuesday 6 October 2026
14:00
```

using the configured Durham week mapping.

---

# Phase 7: Recurrence Optimisation Engine

## Objective

Reduce calendar size while preserving exact dates.

## Rules

### Weekly Series

```text
12,13,14,15,16
```

becomes:

```text
RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY
```

### Fortnightly Series

```text
12,14,16,18
```

becomes:

```text
RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;INTERVAL=2
```

### Irregular Patterns

Split into the smallest sensible recurring groups.

Example:

```text
12,13,14,18,20
```

becomes:

* weekly recurrence for 12-14
* fortnightly recurrence for 18-20

### Remaining Cases

Generate individual events.

Correctness is always more important than compression.

---

# Phase 8: ICS Export Redesign

## Objective

Generate standards-compliant calendar files.

## Time Zone

All events must use:

```text
Europe/London
```

rather than UTC timestamps.

## Deterministic UIDs

UIDs should be derived from activity content.

Suggested inputs:

```text
moduleCode
activityCode
day
time
weeks
```

Repeated exports of the same timetable should therefore update existing calendar entries rather than duplicate them.

## Event Location

Use:

```text
LOCATION:<room>
```

Example:

```text
LOCATION:CG93
```

## Event Description

Include:

```text
Module
Module Code
Activity
Room
Staff
Timetable Entry
```

Example:

```text
Module: Statistical Inference II
Module Code: MATH2761
Activity: Lecture
Room: CG93
Staff: Cumming, Dr J A
Timetable Entry: MATH2761/LECT/001
```

---

# Phase 9: Teaching Week Calendar

## Objective

Provide an optional second ICS file.

## Week Start Events

Generate all-day events:

```text
Week 12
Week 13
Week 14
...
```

on the Monday of each teaching week.

## Term End Events

Optional all-day events:

```text
End of Michaelmas Term
End of Epiphany Term
End of Easter Term
```

using dates from the academic-year configuration.

## Export Strategy

Generate a separate ICS file.

Do not merge these markers into the teaching calendar.

---

# Phase 10: User Interface Revision

## Remove

* Default date selector.
* Default duration selector.
* URL-fetch workflow.
* Generic timetable terminology.

## Add

### Academic Year

```text
2026-27
2027-28
...
```

### Naming Mode

```text
Short Module Names
Full Module Names
```

### Calendar Name

Customisable export name.

### Week Calendar Options

```text
Generate week calendar
Include term-end markers
```

## Retain

* Drag-and-drop upload.
* HTML file upload.
* Event preview table.
* ICS download.

---

# Phase 11: Validation Summary

Display a processing summary after parsing.

Example:

```text
Activities parsed: 14

Occurrences generated: 52

Recurring series: 8

Single events: 6

Warnings: 0
```

Warnings should include:

* unknown activity codes,
* missing room information,
* unknown module abbreviations,
* unmapped weeks.

---

# Recommended Development Order

1. Refactor internal activity model.
2. Create academic-year configuration system.
3. Convert Durham parser to activity output.
4. Add activity-code mappings.
5. Add module short-name mappings.
6. Generate dated occurrences.
7. Implement deterministic UIDs.
8. Replace UTC export with Europe/London export.
9. Implement recurrence optimisation.
10. Implement week-calendar export.
11. Update UI.
12. Add validation summary.
13. Perform end-to-end testing against real Durham timetable exports.

---

# Definition of Done

The application is complete when:

* A Durham timetable HTML export can be uploaded.
* The academic year is automatically detected.
* Users can override the academic year.
* Activities are displayed using full or short module names.
* Event locations are exported correctly.
* Recurrence rules are generated where appropriate.
* Events use deterministic UIDs.
* ICS files use Europe/London time.
* An optional teaching-week calendar can be generated.
* Validation statistics are displayed.
* Output imports correctly into common calendar clients.
